Steve Vinter

← Recipes

Creamy Peanut Noodle

with Gingered Green Beans & Lime

SERVINGS: 2(4) | COOK TIME: 30(40) MINS | LESS PREP ⚡

Remember to wash all produce.

Ingredients

  • 6 tbsp Thai peanut sauce
  • 2 tsp tamari
  • 1 tbsp sweet chile sauce
  • 1 oz ginger, peeled and minced
  • 1 garlic clove, peeled and minced
  • 6 oz green beans, trimmed and cut into ¼-inch pieces
  • 10 oz fresh ramen noodles
  • 2 scallions, trimmed and thinly sliced
  • ¼ cup peanuts, roughly chopped
  • 1 lime, cut into wedges

From your kitchen

  • 1 tbsp vegetable oil
  • Salt

Tools

  • Medium saucepan
  • Large nonstick skillet

Ingredients are listed for 2 servings. If you're making 4 servings, please double your ingredients by using both meal kit bags provided.

Directions

  1. Make the peanut sauce — Bring medium saucepan of salted water to a boil for noodles. In medium bowl, stir together Thai peanut sauce, tamari, and sweet chile sauce.

  2. Cook the gingered green beans — Heat 1 tbsp vegetable oil in large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat. Add ginger, garlic, green beans, and a pinch of salt and cook, stirring occasionally, until green beans are crisp-tender, 3 to 4 minutes. Cover to keep warm. (4-serving meal: use 2 tbsp vegetable oil)

  3. Cook the ramen noodles — Add noodles to boiling water and cook until just al dente, 1 to 2 minutes. Drain noodles and rinse under cool water to stop cooking process. Return noodles to saucepan over medium heat. Add peanut sauce and cook, stirring frequently, until sauce thickens slightly, 1 to 2 minutes.

  4. Serve — Divide creamy peanut noodles between bowls. Top with gingered green beans and sprinkle with scallions and peanuts. Serve with lime wedges. Bon appetit!

Recipe from Purple Carrot.

Recipe from Purple Carrot.
